join_order.notes: adsh_code == sub row index; O(1) lookup fy_col[adsh_code] == 2022; applied during single scan

join_order.build: compact_hash_map<uint64_t,double> 65536 slots (1MB) — built during single scan, max aggregation for fy=2022 adsh codes only
join_order.notes: Restricting aggregation to fy=2022 adsh codes is semantically identical to full subquery: n.adsh=m.adsh means the same adsh appears in both, so fy[adsh] is fixed. Map is 1MB — fits in L3. No thread-local maps, no merge.

filters.mechanism: zone_map skip (skips ~394/395 blocks); per-row check on remaining
direct array lookup fy_col[adsh_code]; applied during single scan before hash insert

scan.access_pattern: sequential with zone-map skip; only ~1 block range read (~100K rows, ~1.7MB)

data_structures.fy_array: mmap sub/fy.bin → int16_t[86135]; direct index by adsh_code; 172KB fits in L2
data_structures.max_val_map: single global compact_hash_map, MAP_CAP=65536 (2^16), MAP_MASK=65535; key=uint64_t pack_key(adsh_code,tagver_code), value=double MAX; size=65536×16B=1MB; NO thread-local maps, NO merge
data_structures.candidate_buffer: std::vector<Candidate> collecting (value, adsh_code, tagver_code) for all rows passing uom+tagver+fy filters; ~18913 entries × 12B = ~227KB

parallelism.notes: Only ~1 valid block (~94K rows) passes zone-map filter. Previous 64-thread impl allocated 64×4MB=256MB of anonymous mmap causing ~65K page faults (~80ms overhead). Single-threaded: zero thread-init cost, single 1MB global map (~256 page faults, negligible), no merge step. Actual scan work is <2MB data, completes in <5ms.

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — skips ~394/395 blocks per scan; pure rows contiguous due to (uom_code,ddate) sort
resolve 'pure' string to int8_t code at startup; never hardcode numeric value

aggregation: hash_group_by during single scan; key=(adsh_code<<32|tagver_code), compute MAX(value); restricted to fy=2022 adsh codes for smaller map footprint

output.post_filter: iterate candidate_buffer, keep rows where value == max_val_map[pack_key(adsh_code,tagver_code)]
output.sort: std::sort on post-filtered candidates (expected ~500 rows), then take top 100

name.timing: deferred — decode only for final 100 result rows

tag.timing: deferred — decode only for final 100 result rows

optimization_notes: Root cause: the iter_0 plan allocated 64 thread-local hash maps of 4MB each (256MB total anonymous mmap), triggering ~65,536 page faults during parallel initialization — this unaccounted overhead consumed ~80ms of the 90ms total, while actual scan work was only 5ms. Additionally, the two-pass design forced two sequential scans of the same ~100K-row block. Fix (Category A+D): collapse to a single-threaded single-pass design. One scan over the ~1 valid block simultaneously builds a 1MB global MAX map (65536 slots) keyed on (adsh_code, tagver_code) and collects candidates — both restricted to fy[adsh_code]==2022 rows, which is semantically equivalent to the full subquery because n.adsh=m.adsh pins each outer row to one adsh_code with fixed fy. Post-scan, candidates are post-filtered against the max map (expected ~500 pass), sorted, and top-100 decoded. Single-threaded eliminates all thread-init and merge overhead; 1MB map generates negligible page faults; one scan instead of two halves memory traffic.
